# Review: `deepmind/993.lean` — Erdős Problem 993

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 993`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `[erdosproblems.com/993](https://www.erdosproblems.com/993)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (independent set sequence unimodality for trees/forests).
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[AMSE87]` with full citation.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos993 ... end Erdos993`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos993`.
- [x] `open SimpleGraph` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `numIndepSets` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Not marked `noncomputable` — correct, since all instances (`Fintype`, `DecidableEq`, `DecidableRel`) are provided and the computation is decidable.
- [x] No `sorry` in the definition.
- [x] `numIndepSets` uses `lowerCamelCase` — correct for a function returning `ℕ`.
- [x] No local notation needed; none used. (N/A)
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity check for the new definition. **Minor/optional** — the checklist says "consider adding" so this is not strictly required.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_993` (snake_case matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references source paper: `[AMSE87]`.

### `answer()` usage

- [x] **Case 3 applies.** The original problem on erdosproblems.com is phrased as a direct assertion: "The independent set sequence of any tree or forest is unimodal." This is not a yes/no question or a request for a value, so no `answer()` wrapper is needed.
- [x] Theorem parameters (`V`, `T`, `hT`) are appropriate for Case 3 (no `answer()` is used, so the linter rule about quantifiers after `answer(sorry)` does not apply).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category research open]` present.
- [x] Value `research open` is valid.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS 5]` attribute (combinatorics).
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (only one number).
- [x] No duplicates.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by` / `sorry` on separate lines (multi-line format, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A — no variants present. The problem has a single conjecture.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `≤`, `¬`, `ℕ`, `⦃ ⦄`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (longest line ~85 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run — **not verified in this review**.
- [ ] Custom linters not run — **not verified in this review**.

---

## Mathematical Correctness Notes

- `numIndepSets` correctly counts the number of independent sets of size `k` by filtering the powerset of all vertices for subsets of the right cardinality with pairwise non-adjacency.
- `hT : T.IsAcyclic` captures forests (acyclic graphs), which includes trees (connected forests). This matches the problem statement "tree or forest".
- The unimodality formulation (∃ m, non-decreasing up to m and non-increasing from m onward) is standard and correct.

## Verdict

**PASS.** The file conforms to all applicable checklist items. Build verification was not performed.

    16→
    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 993
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/993](https://www.erdosproblems.com/993)
    23→
    24→A question of Alavi, Malde, Schwenk, and Erdős [AMSE87], who showed that the independent set
    25→sequence is not constrained for general graphs (in fact every possible pattern of inequalities
    26→is achieved by some graph).
    27→
    28→[AMSE87] Alavi, Y., Malde, P. J., Schwenk, A. J., and Erdős, P., *The vertex independence
    29→sequence of a graph is not constrained*. Congressus Numerantium (1987), 58, 15-23.
    30→-/
    31→
    32→open SimpleGraph
    33→
    34→namespace Erdos993
    35→
    36→/-- The number of independent sets of size $k$ in a simple graph $G$.
    37→An independent set is a set of vertices that are pairwise non-adjacent. -/
    38→def numIndepSets {V : Type*} [Fintype V] [DecidableEq V]
    39→    (G : SimpleGraph V) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (k : ℕ) : ℕ :=
    40→  (Finset.univ.powerset.filter (fun s : Finset V =>
    41→    s.card = k ∧ ∀ ⦃u⦄, u ∈ s → ∀ ⦃v⦄, v ∈ s → u ≠ v → ¬G.Adj u v)).card
    42→
    43→/--
    44→Erdős Problem 993 [AMSE87]:
    45→
    46→The independent set sequence of any tree or forest is unimodal. That is, if
    47→$i_k(T)$ counts the number of independent sets of vertices of size $k$ in a
    48→forest $T$, then there exists $m \geq 0$ such that
    49→$$
    50→i_0(T) \leq i_1(T) \leq \cdots \leq i_m(T) \geq i_{m+1}(T) \geq i_{m+2}(T) \geq \cdots
    51→$$
    52→-/
    53→@[category research open, AMS 5]
    54→theorem erdos_993 {V : Type*} [Fintype V] [DecidableEq V]
    55→    (T : SimpleGraph V) [DecidableRel T.Adj] (hT : T.IsAcyclic) :
    56→    ∃ m : ℕ, (∀ i j : ℕ, i ≤ j → j ≤ m → numIndepSets T i ≤ numIndepSets T j) ∧
    57→             (∀ i j : ℕ, m ≤ i → i ≤ j → numIndepSets T j ≤ numIndepSets T i) := by
    58→  sorry
    59→
    60→end Erdos993
    61→

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 993

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/993](https://www.erdosproblems.com/993)

A question of Alavi, Malde, Schwenk, and Erdős [AMSE87], who showed that the independent set
sequence is not constrained for general graphs (in fact every possible pattern of inequalities
is achieved by some graph).

[AMSE87] Alavi, Y., Malde, P. J., Schwenk, A. J., and Erdős, P., *The vertex independence
sequence of a graph is not constrained*. Congressus Numerantium (1987), 58, 15-23.
-/

open SimpleGraph

namespace Erdos993

/-- The number of independent sets of size $k$ in a simple graph $G$.
An independent set is a set of vertices that are pairwise non-adjacent. -/
def numIndepSets {V : Type*} [Fintype V] [DecidableEq V]
    (G : SimpleGraph V) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (k : ℕ) : ℕ :=
  (Finset.univ.powerset.filter (fun s : Finset V =>
    s.card = k ∧ ∀ ⦃u⦄, u ∈ s → ∀ ⦃v⦄, v ∈ s → u ≠ v → ¬G.Adj u v)).card

/--
Erdős Problem 993 [AMSE87]:

The independent set sequence of any tree or forest is unimodal. That is, if
$i_k(T)$ counts the number of independent sets of vertices of size $k$ in a
forest $T$, then there exists $m \geq 0$ such that
$$
i_0(T) \leq i_1(T) \leq \cdots \leq i_m(T) \geq i_{m+1}(T) \geq i_{m+2}(T) \geq \cdots
$$
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_993 {V : Type*} [Fintype V] [DecidableEq V]
    (T : SimpleGraph V) [DecidableRel T.Adj] (hT : T.IsAcyclic) :
    ∃ m : ℕ, (∀ i j : ℕ, i ≤ j → j ≤ m → numIndepSets T i ≤ numIndepSets T j) ∧
             (∀ i j : ℕ, m ≤ i → i ≤ j → numIndepSets T j ≤ numIndepSets T i) := by
  sorry

end Erdos993

content.text: The review verdict is **PASS**. No changes needed.
